qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Callback name injection in call_user_func_array enables attacker-controlled JavaScript execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets eval interpolation in src/php/funchand/call_user_func_array.js, not the advisory's parse_str forbidden-key check bypass in src/php/strings/parse_str.js.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: inet_pton crashes on malformed overlong IPv6 input instead of rejecting it
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets inet_pton's malformed IPv6 padding path in src/php/network/inet_pton.js, unrelated to the advisory's parse_str prototype-pollution bypass.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is about prototype pollution in locutus.php.strings.parse_str when its forbidden-key check relies on attacker-bypassable String.prototype.includes behavior, while the two qualifying findings concern unrelated helpers.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes prototype pollution in locutus.php.strings.parse_str and points to src/php/strings/parse_str.js lines 77-79, where key.includes('__proto__'|'constructor'|'prototype') is used as the guard.
The preserved session checklist records parse_str as cleared before materialization, so no final qualifying finding was produced for that code path.
call-user-func-array-eval-injection is a separate eval injection in src/php/funchand/call_user_func_array.js with its own reproduced PoC.
inet-pton-ipv6-crash is a separate malformed-input RangeError in src/php/network/inet_pton.js with its own reproduced PoC.
